items.title: Splunk AI Toolkit Vulnerability Enables Arbitrary OS Command Execution Attacks
items.description:
Splunk has disclosed a critical security vulnerability in its AI Toolkit that could allow attackers to execute arbitrary operating system commands on affected systems. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20266, has been assigned a CVSS score of 9.1, highlighting its severe impact on enterprise environments. It affects Splunk AI Toolkit versions below 5.7.4 and is categorized [] 
 The post Splunk AI Toolkit Vulnerability Enables Arbitrary OS Command Execution Attacks appeared first on Cyber S

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Splunk AI Toolkit Vulnerability Enables Arbitrary OS Command Execution Attacks
id: 33daa0a2-4557-5323-81fd-63d0f0f84cbd
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-20266 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: CMDINJECT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-20266
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/18
modified: 2026/06/18
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-20266 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-18T15:46:05Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-20266", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-20266 Exploitation Attempt - CMDINJECT"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1086845; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-18, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2025-10560 - Hardcoded cloud credentials in Worksnaps client application binaries expose production cloud resources
items.description:
CVE ID : CVE-2025-10560
 
 Published : June 18, 2026, 8:32 a.m. | 2hours, 33minutes ago
 
 Description : Worksnaps before version 1.6.20260201 contains hardcoded cloud credentials and related secret material in the Worksnaps client application binaries. The exposed credentials included AWS access keys, S3 bucket names, and related cloud access information. The originally exposed AWS credentials authenticated as the AWS account root identity and provided access to Worksnaps production

iocs.value: DNS beaconing: regular 60-second interval queries to single domain

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2025-10560 - Hardcoded cloud credentials in Worksnaps client application bin
id: 884c9f1a-9424-5d1a-9bd4-be51411e8427
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2025-10560 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-10560
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/18
modified: 2026/06/18
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2025-10560 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-18T15:46:05Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2025-10560", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2025-10560 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6890020; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-18, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2026-8811 - Path traversal in PDF generation module
items.description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-8811
 
 Published : June 18, 2026, 9:05 a.m. | 2hours ago
 
 Description : SEPPmail versions before 15.0.5 allow improper handling of attachment filenames during encrypted PDF generation. An attacker can exploit this to create new files outside the intended directory, potentially placing files in web-accessible locations.
 
 Severity: 7.1 | HIGH
 
 Visit the link for more details, such as CVSS details, affected products, timeline, and more...

items.title: CVE-2026-2021 - Slideshow Gallery LITE <= 1.8.5 - Authenticated (Contributor+) Stored Cross-Site Scripting via 'alwaysauto' Shortcode Attribute
items.description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-2021
 
 Published : June 18, 2026, 8:31 a.m. | 2hours, 34minutes ago
 
 Description : The Slideshow Gallery LITE plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'alwaysauto' shortcode attribute in all versions up to, and including, 1.8.5. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user-supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitra

items.title: CVE-2026-50643 - OutofBounds Read in 8cc
items.description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-50643
 
 Published : June 18, 2026, 8:58 a.m. | 2hours, 7minutes ago
 
 Description : 8cc is vulnerable to an OutofBounds Read due to improper handling of #line directives and GNU linemarkers. The compiler accepts attacker-controlled filename and line number metadata and later uses it without validation when accessing source line arrays.
By supplying invalid or oversized line numbers, an attacker can trigger out-of-bounds memory access and a crash.

Maintainer of t

items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-50643 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-18T15:46:05Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5

| order by TimeGenerated desc

items.title: BBOT: Symlink-Following Arbitrary Write via github_workflows Module
items.description: The `github_workflows` module constructs local directory paths from user-controlled repository names without validating for symlinks. A local attacker sharing the scan directory can plant a symlink at the predictable output path, causing workflow data to be written to an attacker-chosen location.
